question_text: A hospital uses different types of electromagnetic radiation for medical imaging. X-rays are used to detect broken bones, while infrared cameras are used to detect heat patterns in tissue. Explain why X-rays are more suitable than infrared radiation for imaging inside the body, and identify one health danger associated with repeated X-ray exposure.
correct_answer: X-rays have a shorter wavelength/higher frequency than infrared, allowing them to penetrate through skin and tissue to image bones. X-rays are ionising radiation that can damage cells/DNA and increase cancer risk with repeated exposure.
explanation: This tests understanding that different EM waves have different properties suited to different uses. X-rays can pass through soft tissue but infrared cannot, making them ideal for medical imaging. However, X-rays are dangerous because they can ionise atoms in cells, causing damage. Students must compare two applications and identify a specific health hazard.
